title: Processing tar file(no such file or directory): exit status 1
body:
### Issue Description

When trying an windows image from an ACR.
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:edbd72df76b46e904108d2f61a63c295b3e3d8092dbd5a03bbeb2fb4d34a3e55": processing tar file(no such file or directory): exit status 1

I tried other images in that ACR and those are working fine.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  pull image windows from an registry
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:edbd72df76b46e904108d2f61a63c295b3e3d8092dbd5a03bbeb2fb4d34a3e55": processing tar file(no such file or directory): exit status 1

### Describe the results you expected

I want to pull the image.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Can you share the image? Is it only one specific image or does it fail for all images?

It is an private image, it is an windows image. I tested with docker and then it can pull.
I also tested with podman on a other image in that acr but that worked.
Can you create a reproducer that we can use? How did you build such image? Can you pull it with docker? If not it could be that your image is corrupted.

Image is an old image, where the dockerfile is lost.
And yes i can pull it with docker. Then it works
"windows image" do you mean it contains Microsoft Windows code? Podman does not support Windows Containers.
@Danny-de-bree, is the image for Windows containers?  Which format is the image in?  Is it some kind of OCI artifact?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we got no response, closing.

Hi @rhatdan,
Please reopen. I can reproduce the same issue with my private image in a gitlab registry. If you let me have your gitlab account I can grant you access.

For me it looks like this. I don't want to run the image, but I want to retag it. But I can't since I can't pull... LOL

Actually with another windows image a wanted to change the CMD without adding additional files and I wanted to do some quashing.

@rhatdan 
Hi again,
I noticed the same happens on the windows/server image! So this must be somehow a podman bug. PLease let me know if you can reproduce.

```
[root@localhost ~]# podman pull mcr.microsoft.com/windows/server:ltsc2022 --os windows
Trying to pull mcr.microsoft.com/windows/server:ltsc2022...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4d2b02711923 done   | 
Copying blob 7d7d659851e2 skipped: already exists  
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:4d2b02711923554609a35820e8ff0d6928da3214a427efac9fa49df84f23c19f": processing tar file(no such file or directory): exit status 1
```

Update.... The same happens for podman for windows

```
PS C:\Users\bjoerndieding> podman pull mcr.microsoft.com/windows/server:ltsc2022 --os windows
Trying to pull mcr.microsoft.com/windows/server:ltsc2022...

Copying blob sha256:7d7d659851e26310f1a29af00e4763f6519162a8621df8923129f1385754e79b
Error: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:4d2b02711923554609a35820e8ff0d6928da3214a427efac9fa49df84f23c19f": processing tar file(no such file or directory): exit status 1
```
